task.title: Manual character and chat memory entry

coreClaim: A user can manually add a durable memory from the character Memories tab or the current chat Memory Console, and each write stays with the owner shown in the UI.

inScope: Inline UI feedback and owner changes

claimBoundary.coreClaim: Character-panel writes create character-scoped canonical memories; Memory Console writes create chat-scoped embedded rows; neither unsaved draft follows a changed owner.
currentPathsOrFormats: canonical-memories rows with character scope and memory-index-rows lexical indexes
chats.memories rows with canonicalMemoryVersion 1, manual kind, chat scope, and lexical embedding
legacyPathsOrFormats: Existing canonical character memories remain unchanged
Existing chats.memories rows remain unchanged and the new row is appended
Chats without manually created memories keep their current behavior
outOfScopeClaims: No automatic capture or prompt behavior is changed.
No live native-app visual pass is claimed.

contractLaneGate.brokenContract: src/features exposed memory management but no owner-specific create action; chat writes also needed privileged src-tauri persistence and remote parity.
contractLaneGate.producer: src/features owner-specific composers through src/shared/api focused wrappers
contractLaneGate.consumer: src-tauri chat memory storage and canonical character-memory storage, then existing recall consumers
contractLaneGate.impliedContract: src/features selects the visible owner, src/shared/api transports the request, and src-tauri or the canonical memory capability owns durable storage and indexing.
contractLaneGate.actualEnforcement: Character writes build a character-scoped CanonicalMemoryInput and rebuild only that character index; chat writes build and embed one chat-scoped row before one append patch; HTTP dispatch calls the same Rust function.
contractLaneGate.primaryOwnerDetail: src-features owns editor state, src/shared/api owns command transport, and src-tauri owns privileged chat persistence while the existing canonical-memory capability owns character persistence.
contractLaneGate.wrongLaneFixToAvoid: Do not write chats.memories directly from React, infer ownership in the remote wrapper, or duplicate the Rust persistence contract in the UI.
regressionProof: character-memory-model.spec.ts proves exact character ownership and manual provenance
CharacterMemoriesTab.spec.tsx proves trimmed submission, empty rejection, accessibility, and owner-change reset
MemoryRecallMemoriesModal.component.spec.tsx proves the same chat-local UI invariants
chat_memory.rs proves an embedded append, unchanged existing row, and empty rejection
http_dispatch.rs proves remote dispatch reaches the shared capability

positiveRows.claim: A character-panel entry becomes a character-scoped canonical memory and refreshes that character's lexical index.
positiveRows.evidence: character-memory-model.spec.ts and CharacterMemoriesTab.spec.tsx pass; pnpm check:architecture confirms the boundary.

positiveRows.claim: A Memory Console entry becomes one embedded chat-local memory through desktop and remote paths.
positiveRows.evidence: chat-command-api.spec.ts plus the focused Rust manual_memory_creation and dispatch_creates_manual_chat_memory_through_the_shared_capability tests pass.

contradictionRows.claim: Whitespace-only content must not create a memory.
contradictionRows.evidence: Both UI component suites disable empty submission; the character model and Rust storage tests reject whitespace-only content.

contradictionRows.claim: An unsaved draft must not appear under a different character or chat.
contradictionRows.evidence: CharacterMemoriesTab.spec.tsx and MemoryRecallMemoriesModal.component.spec.tsx rerender with a new owner and prove the composer resets.

legacyDefaultRows.claim: Adding a chat memory must not mutate an existing chat-memory row.
legacyDefaultRows.evidence: manual_memory_creation seeds an existing row, creates a new row, and asserts the original id and content are unchanged.

legacyDefaultRows.claim: Existing memory management and inherited character-memory behavior remain valid.
legacyDefaultRows.evidence: The existing MemoryRecallMemoriesModal.spec.ts suite and full pnpm check pass unchanged.

untestedRows: No native-app screenshot or manual click-through was captured; focused component tests and the production build cover the changed UI contract.

ownedFacts.description: Manual chat memories are persisted with chat scope and a lexical embedding before the chat row is patched.
ownedFacts.evidence: manual_memory_creation asserts scopeType chat, scopeId chat-1, hasEmbedding true, embeddingSource lexical, and persisted equality.

ownedFacts.description: Remote and desktop chat-memory creation share one Rust capability.
ownedFacts.evidence: The HTTP dispatch test invokes chat_memory_create and asserts the returned row is the persisted row; architecture dispatch validation passes.

userActionCopy.description: Users who want a separate backup can export memories before editing or deleting them.
userActionCopy.source: Character Editor > Memories or Chat Settings > Continuity > Memory Console
userActionCopy.destination: A user-selected folder outside De-Koi application data
files: <character-name>-memories.json or the Memory Recall export file
userActionCopy.action: Use the existing Export control and save or copy the resulting JSON file into the selected backup folder.
detectedLayouts: Character memories use the existing de_koi_character_memories export envelope.
Chat memories use the existing Memory Recall export envelope.
This PR does not change current or legacy export layouts.

reviewThreadLedger.finding: Local Bunny review found unsaved composer drafts could follow a changed chat or character owner.
reviewThreadLedger.fix: Reset each composer when its owner id changes and added negative component tests for both surfaces.

negativeRowsTested: Whitespace-only chat memory with unchanged persisted row count

notes: Bunny local review passed after the owner-change draft finding was fixed.
The user explicitly authorized PR creation and merge after hosted gates pass.
